content.problemStatement:
Let's call a string XXX interesting if it satisfies the following conditions:


XXX contains at least KKK pairwise distinct characters
for any two characters c1c_1c1​ and c2c_2c2​ which appear in XXX, the frequency (number of occurrences) of c1c_1c1​ in XXX is equal to the frequency of c2c_2c2​ in XXX
You are given a string SSS. Compute the number of interesting substrings of SSS. Two substrings are considered different if they are located at different positions in SSS.
constraints: the sum of |S| in all test cases does not exceed 14,000
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The first and only line of each test case contains a string S, followed by a space and an integer K.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of interesting substrings.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a sliding window approach along with frequency counting. We will maintain a frequency map of characters in the current window and check if the window is interesting by verifying if all characters in the window have the same frequency and if the number of distinct characters is at least K.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * |S|^2) — for each test case, we are iterating over all possible substrings of S, and for each substring, we are counting the frequency of characters.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(|S|) — we need to store the frequency map of characters in the current window, which can have at most |S| characters.
